10× gitagent v0.1.0
why playground tools memory ★ GitHub
v0.1.0 · 101 commits · 391 tests · 17 tools · MIT

Agents that live in your repo,
defined as files.

gitagent is the open-source framework for declaring persistent, versioned AI agents as .github/agents/<name>.md files in any GitHub repository. They run on real events. They remember. They ship the work.

Star on GitHub ./try-it → Read the docs
13 agents online 391 tests passing 0 deps on frameworks 100% auditable
01 · live demo

Run a real agent.
In 5 seconds.

A real gitagent command, run against a real GitHub issue. Watch the agent think, decide, and ship — all in your terminal.

~/projects/gitagent — zsh — 80×24
REC 00:00
02 · manifest playground

Edit the manifest.
See the agent change.

Every agent is just a YAML file. Edit it. The preview updates in real-time. No build step, no compiler, no framework to learn.

.github/agents/issue-triage.md yaml + markdown
live preview ● valid
✓ manifest parsed · 4 tools · 1 trigger · git memory
name
issue-triage
triggers
[issues.opened]
model
anthropic / claude-sonnet-4-5 · temp 0.1
memory
git · path: .gitagent/memory · semantic: true
tools
[github.add_labels, github.post_comment, memory.read, memory.write]
approval
read: never · write: required
limits
maxSteps: 8 · timeoutMs: 60000
permissions
closeIssues: false · mergePRs: false · release: false
03 · the pitch

The hard part isn't the agent.
It's the integration.

The agent is 20% of the work. The integration is 80%. gitagent makes the 80% boring — so the 20% is all you have to think about.

// 01

Declarative, not imperative

The agent is a YAML file. You describe what you want. The runtime does the rest. No state machines to design, no event loops to debug.

// 02

Versioned, like code

Every agent is in your repo. Every change is a commit. Every change is a PR. The agent's history is git history.

// 03

Auditable, like infra

Every action is logged. Every decision is traced. Every tool call is recorded. The agent is observable end-to-end.

// 04

Recoverable, like backups

Every action is reversible. Every state is in git. The agent can be undone, rolled back, and restored. Always.

04 · in the wild

13 agents. One repo.
Zero coordination.

gitagent is the framework behind mr10xdev-blog — a real production site where 13 agents share a single repository and ship the work in parallel.

10× gitagent the runtime
doc
push.main
changelog
release.published
meta
push.main
release
release.published
oncall
schedule.daily
weekly-summary
schedule.weekly
triage
issues.opened
welcome
issues.opened
refactor
pull_request.opened
standup
schedule.daily
docs release support ops
05 · the 17 tools

Out of the box.
No boilerplate.

17 production-ready GitHub tools. Zod-validated inputs. Retry with backoff. Dry-run support. Composable into skills.

→
post_comment
Reply to issues/PRs
→
add_labels
Tag with one or more labels
→
remove_label
Remove a single label
→
create_issue
File a new issue
→
update_issue
Edit title/body/state
→
close_issue
Close with reason
→
reopen_issue
Reopen a closed one
→
create_pr
Open a new pull request
→
merge_pr
Merge with strategy
→
request_review
Ask humans for review
→
add_reaction
React to issues/comments
→
get_file
Read a file from the repo
→
list_issues
Search & filter issues
→
list_pull_requests
List PRs by state
→
list_workflow_runs
See CI history
→
memory.read
Recall past state
→
memory.write
Remember for next time
06 · memory

Four levels.
One hierarchy.

gitagent ships four memory backends, each tuned for a different access pattern. Use them in any combination.

LAYER L4

SemanticMemory

Finds similar memories via embedding similarity. For "what did the user ask about last time?" queries.

vector
LAYER L3

EpisodicMemory

Append-only log of past events and decisions. For "what did I do last time?" queries.

history
LAYER L2

SqliteMemory

Persistent single-file memory with prefix queries. For high-volume agent runs.

indexed
LAYER L1

GitMemory

Files in your repo, committed on every write. Memory is auditable in git history.

versioned
07 · the test suite

391 tests.
All green.

Every public API is covered. Every tool is tested. Every error path is exercised. The CI is the source of truth.

391 / 391 Tests passing on Node 22 · 100% typecheck clean
manifest ● 47 tests
runtime ● 38 tests
tools ● 96 tests
memory ● 41 tests
server ● 47 tests
providers ● 28 tests
cli ● 12 tests
skills ● 13 tests
observability ● 6 tests
examples ● 2 tests
errors ● 11 tests
matcher ● 7 tests
loader ● 8 tests
events ● 8 tests
registry ● 5 tests
token ● 6 tests
08 · get started

From zero to agent
in 90 seconds.

Three commands. One file. Your agent is live, deployed, and answering issues on GitHub. 

# clone, install, build
$ git clone https://github.com/10xdev4u-alt/gitagent
$ cd gitagent && pnpm install
$ pnpm test   # 391 tests · should all pass
# .github/agents/triage.md
---
name: triage
triggers:
  - issues.opened
model:
  provider: anthropic
  name: claude-sonnet-4-5
tools:
  - github.add_labels
  - github.post_comment
---

# You are an issue triager. Label each new issue
# as bug, feature, question, or duplicate.
# start the webhook server (or deploy to GitHub Actions)
$ npx gitagent serve --port 3000

  ╭───────────────────────────────────────╮
  │  ●  gitagent v0.1.0              │
  │  ●  13 agents loaded              │
  │  ●  17 tools registered            │
  │  ●  391 tests passing              │
  │  ●  listening on :3000           │
  ╰───────────────────────────────────────╯

# Your agent is live. Open an issue and watch it work.
gitagent v0.1.0

Open-source framework for declaring persistent, versioned AI agents as files in your GitHub repository. MIT licensed.
